sources: FOMC post-meeting statements 1999-2026, corpus-validated rate-action verbs

excluded.increas: mostly 'increase in costs' / 'increase in the discount rate', not the funds-rate target (confounded)
excluded.reduc: mixes rate reductions with QT 'reducing its holdings' (confounded)
excluded.cut: appears as 'cut back' production/spending, not rate cuts
excluded.hike: 0 hits - not used in FOMC statement prose

notes: DESCRIPTIVE / MONITORING ONLY - NOT predictive. Pure rate-ACTION verbs: 'raise' / 'lower its target for the federal funds rate'. By construction this index mirrors the policy DECISION itself, so it is redundant with any rate-surprise control and carries NO marginal out-of-sample value (see docs/results/2026-06-29-lexicon-baseline-verdict.md). Built because the predictive stance lexicon (hawk_dove.json) goes silent on 2024+ statements, which express stance only through the action verb. Use for a hike/cut/hold tracker; never feed it to the marginal-effect test.
